page: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 server family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The configuration provides 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU features 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic accelerators like AES and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of memory per core. It contains no local storage or GPUs, but is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance, gzip single-threaded compression, and bzip3 single-threaded decompression all rank in the top 10%. Multi-threaded performance is also robust, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and PassMark CPU metrics placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core stress-ng performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the average tier. For machine learning workloads, the server demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models, though performance drops to the bottom 10% when executing text generation on large 70B models due to the absence of dedicated GPU hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64-32as_v7 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, reducing base costs for workloads reliant on external network storage. This server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, data caching layers, and memory-heavy development or build pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Easv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 4.5 GHz clock speed, paired with 512.0 GB of RAM. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities. However, multi-core stress-ng and memory bandwidth metrics remain average, and large-model LLM text generation is poor due to the lack of hardware acceleration. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-bound workloads, making it an appropriate fit for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without excessive virtual CPU overhead.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC processor and 512 GB of RAM. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, built for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processing and substantial RAM capacity for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing 16.0 GB of memory per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPUs, with support for Premium Storage
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
